seoTitle: How to Download a File in n8n Using HTTP Request
metaDescription: Learn how to download binary files like images or PDFs in n8n using the HTTP Request node set to binary response mode.
intro: Handling files like images, PDFs, or spreadsheets in n8n requires switching a node's response format from standard JSON to binary mode. This guide covers setting up the HTTP Request node to fetch a file from an external URL, assigning it a binary property key, and passing it along to downstream nodes.

steps.heading: Open your target workflow
steps.body: Load the automation canvas where you want to add the file download step.

steps.heading: Add an HTTP Request node
steps.body: Drop the HTTP Request node onto the canvas to begin the file fetch setup.

steps.heading: Enter the source file URL
steps.body: Input the external HTTPS URL of the file you want to download and set the request method.

steps.heading: Switch the response format to binary
steps.body: In the node's options, change the response format from JSON to binary file delivery.

steps.heading: Assign a binary property key
steps.body: Name a specific binary property key to hold the extracted file stream so downstream nodes can reference it.

steps.heading: Run and verify the download
steps.body: Execute the node and open the binary preview tab to confirm the file size, mime type, and content are correct.

faq.question: Why isn't my downloaded file showing up correctly?
faq.answer: This is usually because the node's response format is still set to JSON instead of binary — switching that setting typically fixes it.

faq.question: Can I download any type of file this way?
faq.answer: Yes, the HTTP Request node in binary mode can handle images, PDFs, spreadsheets, and other file types from a public URL.

faq.question: What can I do with the downloaded file afterward?
faq.answer: You can pipe it into storage nodes, attach it to an outgoing email, or upload it to a cloud drive as part of the same workflow.

faq.question: How do I check that the file downloaded correctly?
faq.answer: Use the node's binary preview tab after execution to verify the data size and mime type match what you expected.
